zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Grey於大約1283年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾洛--也時，她的父親Baron John de Grey26歲，母親Maud de Verdun28歲。她和Ralph Basset至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1353年4月5日在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Grey was born about 1283, in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Baron John de Grey, was 26 and her mother, Maud de Verdun, was 28. She had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Ralph Basset. She died on 5 April 1353, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Grey(이)가 대략 1283년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 로스--w예에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron John de Grey(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Verdun(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Ralph Basset(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1353년 4월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Grey родилась около 1283 г. в местности росс-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Baron John de Grey было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Verdun, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Ralph Basset. Она умерла 5 апреля 1353 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Grey nació aproximadamente 1283, en Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baron John de Grey, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Maud de Verdun, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Ralph Basset. Murió el 5 de abril de 1353, en Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años.
